---
title: "Home"
---

<img src="headshot.jpg" alt="Headshot of Silvia-Jessica Mostacedo-Marasovic" style="width:180px; float:right; margin-left:20px; margin-bottom:10px; border-radius:50%;">

## Dr. Silvia-Jessica Mostacedo-Marasovic

**Discipline-Based Education Research | Water Systems | Sustainability Education | Decision-Making**

I am a discipline-based education research (DBER) scholar studying how learners develop the capacity to understand and make decisions about **complex socio-environmental systems**, particularly water resources.
My research program integrates **water literacy, systems thinking, stakeholder reasoning, structured decision-making, and learning analytics** to investigate how students and educators reason about shared natural resources under conditions of uncertainty.
I develop and study instructional approaches that help learners: understand water systems as interconnected human–environment systems, evaluate competing stakeholder perspectives and tradeoffs, and use evidence, models, and data to support decision-making.
My work spans undergraduate education, teacher and faculty professional development, environmental literacy, and sustainability education.

## Current Appointment
- **Postdoctoral Research Associate**
- University of Texas at Arlington (2024 - Present)
- Departments: Higher Education, Adult Learning and Organizational Studies & Earth and Environmental Sciences

## Education
- **Ph.D.**, Earth and Environmental Sciences — University of Texas at Arlington (2024)
- **M.S.**, Sustainable Development Practice — University of Florida (2020)
- **B.S.**, Socio-Economic Development and Environment — Zamorano University (2008)

## Featured Funding
**National Science Foundation (NSF IUSE; DUE No. 2517117)** — **Co-Principal Investigator**

*Enhancing Undergraduate Education About Stakeholder Competition and Cooperation in Water Systems Using Game Theory*  

Principal Investigator: Cory T. Forbes | Awarded: $403,471 | 2025–2028

This project investigates undergraduate learning about water resources management, stakeholder reasoning, structured decision-making, and game theory in socio-environmental systems.

## Contact
- Email: [silvajessica.mostacedomaraso@uta.edu](mailto:silvajessica.mostacedomaraso@uta.edu)
- Google Scholar: [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=cLUXu38AAAAJ)
- ORCID: [0000-0001-6860-719X](https://orcid.org/0000-0001-6860-719X)
- LinkedIn: [linkedin.com/in/jessicamostacedo](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jessicamostacedo)
